examples: They are minded, also, to establish the idolatrous forms of English Episcopacy.
Since the poem's initial appearance, the institution of episcopacy, or church hierarchy, has been seriously eroded.
By 1612, James had succeeded in carrying out the first part of his policy, the re-establishment of diocesan episcopacy.
But the pope promptly made him responsible for relations between Poland and the Vatican, while another liberal got his job at the Episcopacy.
In May conservatives lost another battle, to have one of their own named secretary of the Polish Episcopacy, the church's chief manager and spokesman.
